---
title: "R Notebook"
output: html_notebook
---

```{r message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(ggpubr)
library(vegan)
library(llnl)
library(phyloseq)
```

# Functions
```{r}
align_dists <- function(dist1, dist2) {
  # Convert dist objects to matrices
  mat1 <- as.matrix(dist1)
  mat2 <- as.matrix(dist2)
  
  # Find shared sample names
  shared_samples <- intersect(rownames(mat1), rownames(mat2))
  
  # If no shared samples, stop
  if (length(shared_samples) == 0) {
    stop("No shared samples between the two distance matrices.")
  }
  
  # Subset and order both matrices
  mat1_subset <- mat1[shared_samples, shared_samples]
  mat2_subset <- mat2[shared_samples, shared_samples]
  
  # Convert back to dist objects
  dist1_aligned <- as.dist(mat1_subset)
  dist2_aligned <- as.dist(mat2_subset)
  
  # Return as a named list
  return(list(dist1 = dist1_aligned, dist2 = dist2_aligned))
}

align_and_melt_dists <- function(...) {
  df.list <- list(...)
  
  long_dfs <- mapply(function(mat, name) {
    as.matrix(mat) %>% 
    as.data.frame(.) %>%
      rownames_to_column("A") %>%
      pivot_longer(-A, names_to = "B", values_to = name)
  }, df.list, names(df.list), SIMPLIFY = FALSE)
  
  # Join all melted dfs together (assumes they are compatible in structure)
  combined_df <- reduce(long_dfs, full_join, by = c("A", "B"))
  
  return(combined_df)
}

```

# Data
```{r}
dist.16s <- readRDS("../Data/amplicon/rarefied_phyloseq_object.king.RDS")
dist.16s <- data.frame(t(otu_table(dist.16s)))
dist.16s <- llnl::dist.table(dist.16s,"Y","bray")
dist.16s <- as.matrix(dist.16s)
rownames(dist.16s) <- gsub("_16S|Dry_Soil", "", rownames(dist.16s))
colnames(dist.16s) <- gsub("_16S|Dry_Soil", "", colnames(dist.16s))
dist.16s <- as.dist(dist.16s)

# dist.virome <- readRDS("../Data/virome.dist.BC.RDS") %>% 
#   as.dist()

dist.virome <- read.csv("../Data/normalized_abundance_tables/bulk_virome_normalized_table.csv",row.names = 1) %>% 
  llnl::dist.table(.,"Y","bray") %>% 
  as.dist()

dist.metat <- read.csv("../Data/normalized_abundance_tables/active_votu_abundances.csv",row.names = 1) %>%
  rownames_to_column("sample") %>%
  mutate(sample = gsub("\\.R\\.", "", sample),
         sample = gsub("Dry\\.soil", "", sample),
         sample = gsub("\\.", "_", sample),
         sample = gsub("_P", "P", sample)) %>% 
  column_to_rownames("sample")%>% 
  llnl::dist.table(.,"Y","bray")

# Clean column names
colnames(dist.metat) <- colnames(dist.metat) %>%
  gsub("\\.R\\.", "", .) %>%
  gsub("Dry\\.soil", "", .) %>%
  gsub("\\.", "_", .) %>% 
  gsub("_P", "P", .)

rownames(dist.metat) <- rownames(dist.metat) %>%
  gsub("\\.R\\.", "", .) %>%
  gsub("Dry\\.soil", "", .) %>%
  gsub("\\.", "_", .) %>% 
  gsub("_P", "P", .)

dist.metat <- dist.metat %>% 
  as.dist()
```

```{r}
combined <- align_and_melt_dists(
  dist_metat = dist.metat,
  dist_virome = dist.virome,
  dist_16S = dist.16s
)
```


#Mantel test
```{r}
# Make sure the distance objects are in the same order and remove samples not shared between the two
# Virome vs 16s
mantel_results <- bind_rows(
  virome.16s <- align_dists(dist.virome, dist.16s ) %>% 
    {mantel(.$dist1, .$dist2, method = "spearman", permutations = 9999)} %>% 
    {data.frame(statistic = .$statistic, 
                significance = .$signif,
                test = "Virome vs 16S")},
  
  # Metat vs 16s
  metat.16s <- align_dists(dist.metat,dist.16s) %>% 
    {mantel(.$dist1, .$dist2, method = "spearman", permutations = 9999)}%>% 
    {data.frame(statistic = .$statistic, 
                significance = .$signif,
                test = "MetaT vs 16S")},
  
  #MetaT vs Virome 
  metat.virome <- align_dists(dist.metat,dist.virome) %>% 
    {mantel(.$dist1, .$dist2, method = "spearman", permutations = 9999)}%>% 
    {data.frame(statistic = .$statistic, 
                significance = .$signif,
                test = "MetaT vs Virome")}
)
```

# All figures

```{r}
plot_pairwise_dataframe <- function(dist_df) {
  # Identify distance columns
  dist_cols <- setdiff(names(dist_df), c("A", "B")) 
  
  # Get all unique column combinations
  pair_combos <- combn(dist_cols, 2, simplify = FALSE)
  
  # Create a long format with pairs and facet labels
  plot_df <- map_dfr(pair_combos, function(pair) {
    dist_df %>%
      select(all_of(c("A", "B", pair))) %>%
      rename(x = !!pair[1], y = !!pair[2]) %>%
      mutate(comparison = paste(pair[1], "vs", pair[2]),
             comparison = gsub("dist_", "", comparison),
             comparison = gsub("metat", "MetaT", comparison),
             comparison = gsub("virome", "Virome", comparison))
  })
  }

combined.df <- plot_pairwise_dataframe(combined) %>% 
  left_join(mantel_results, by = c("comparison" = "test"))

# Create the plot
ggplot(combined.df, aes(x = x, y = y)) +
  geom_point() +
  facet_wrap(~comparison,scales = "free") +
  geom_text(
    aes(
      x = Inf, y = -Inf,
      label = paste0("Spearman R = ", round(statistic, 2),"\n",
                     "p = ", signif(significance, 2))
    ),
    hjust = 1.1, vjust = -0.5,
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    data = distinct(combined.df, comparison, statistic, significance)
  ) +
  # Add lm line conditionally (only for significant correlations)
  geom_smooth(
    data = filter(combined.df, significance < 0.05),
    method = "lm",
    se = FALSE
  )+
  xlab("Bray-Curtis\ndisimilarity distance 1") +
  ylab("Bray-Curtis\ndisimilarity distance 2")

ggsave("../figures/supplementary_figure4.png", width = 11, height = 9, dpi = 300, units = "in")

```
